events.title: Villager at the First Step
events.text: A villager waves from the first bend of the pilgrim road. "The higher you climb, the friendlier the smiles. Don't trust the teeth."

help.message: The villager laughs too loudly when the bandits run. Your cuts sting; your purse is 30 gold heavier.

ignore.message: You keep climbing. Behind you, the villager's warning turns into a song about teeth.

events.title: Pilgrim-Road Merchant
events.text: A merchant sells Nine-Immortal charms from a lacquered pack. Hu is delighted; the merchant says he never climbs past the second shrine, "as policy."

trade.message: The merchant spreads charms, manuals, and a smile that refuses to look uphill.

leave.message: You leave the souvenirs behind. The merchant wishes you luck and shuts his pack before the mist notices.

events.title: The Hungry Shrine
events.text: An old shrine leans into the path. Its incense is cold, but something inside it asks for more than coin. Giving something up feels strangely light.

gold.message: The shrine swallows the coins without sound. A technique settles in your mind like borrowed weight.

blood.message: Blood darkens the stone. The lesson arrives clean and cold, and you feel lighter than you should.

leave.message: You bow and keep your offerings. The shrine watches your back until the path turns.

events.title: The Drunk Below the Gate
events.text: A shabby drunk sits below the gate, smelling of wine and bamboo. He refuses to teach and laughs at the Nine: "I'm the only master on this mountain who never asks for tuition."

pass.message: The drunk grins through his beard. "Not bad." He gives you one lesson and denies he taught it.

fail.message: The gourd cracks your forehead. "Too stiff," he says, and drinks to your improvement.

decline.message: The drunk shrugs, takes another sip, and waves like he has seen how you die.

events.title: Dropout Camp
events.text: Smoke rises behind the trees. The camp is full of failed outer-gate disciples practicing bad copies of immortal forms.

sneak.message: You slip past while a dropout argues that his Ox stance is supposed to lean that far.

infiltrate.message: You step into the clearing. Their borrowed forms collapse into panic before the first blade is drawn.

events.title: A Founder-Era Scroll
events.text: A scroll lies half-buried in the dirt, older than any school on the mountain. Reading the calligraphy too long makes your eyes ache.

read.message: The founder-era strokes teach your hands before your mind agrees. Something behind the letters stays shut.
